walnut-and-plum-cake-teleculinaria.name: Walnut And Plum Cake
instructions: Start the oven at 180.3. Garnish a form with butter and sprinkle with flour.

In a bowl, beat the butter very well with sugar, until a white cream is left. Add the eggs, one by one and always beating, then add the flour mixed with the yeast and beat. Put on the ground nut and the plums and mix well.
Deite a mistura anterior na forma e leve ao forno, durante 45 minutos. Verifique a cozedura com um palito e se sair seco retire. Deixe arrefecer, desenforme e sirva decorado a gosto.

warm-eggplant-salad-with-walnuts.name: Warm Eggplant Salad With Walnuts

ingredients.prep: halved lengthwise, cut crosswise into 2" sections

instructions: Preheat oven to 350°. Toast walnuts on a rimmed baking sheet, tossing occasionally, until fragrant and slightly darker, 8–10 minutes. Let cool, then finely chop; set aside.
Meanwhile, whisk lemon juice, cinnamon, red pepper, and 1 Tbsp. oil in a large bowl.

Heat remaining ¼ cup oil in a large skillet, preferably nonstick, over medium-high. Add eggplant and cook, tossing occasionally, until golden brown and tender, 7–9 minutes. Using a slotted spoon or tongs, transfer eggplant to bowl with dressing, leaving any oil in pan behind; discard oil. Add onion, mint, and three-quarters of reserved walnuts to bowl. Season with salt and toss to combine.
Transfer eggplant salad to a platter. Drizzle with date syrup, then top with remaining walnuts.

warm-white-bean-dip.name: Warm White Bean Dip
instructions: {'step': '1', 'text': 'Pulse garlic in a food processor until minced.'}
{'step': '2', 'text': 'Add beans, cumin, lime juice, and salt; pulse until all ingredients are well combined, 5 to 10 seconds.'}
{'step': '3', 'text': 'With the motor running, slowly pour olive oil through the feed tube and process until mixture is creamy, about 20 seconds.'}
{'step': '4', 'text': 'Transfer to a microwave-safe serving bowl.'}
{'step': '5', 'text': 'Heat in the microwave on high for 30 seconds.'}

watermelon-grapefruit-agua-fresca.name: Watermelon & Grapefruit Agua Fresca

ingredients.prep: ruby red, or pink grapefruit juice

instructions: Working in batches, purée watermelon in a blender until smooth.
Pour into a large bowl and let stand for 10 minutes.
Skim foam from surface and discard.
Set a fine-mesh sieve over a large pitcher; line sieve with cheesecloth.
Strain purée into pitcher (you should have almost 7 cups juice).
Stir in grapefruit juice.
Fill glasses with ice.
Divide agua fresca among glasses.

weeknight-beans-on-toast.name: Weeknight Beans On Toast

ingredients.prep: cut in half crosswise, split lengthwise, or 2 ciabatta rolls, split lengthwise

instructions: Heat oil in a large Dutch oven or high-sided skillet over medium-high. Add sausage and press down on it with a heatproof rubber spatula to flatten slightly. Cook, undisturbed, until browned underneath, about 4 minutes. Turn sausage over and cook, undisturbed, until browned on the other side, about 3 minutes. Break up sausage into bite-size pieces with spatula. Add sliced garlic, oregano, and red pepper flakes (if using) and cook, stirring, until garlic is beginning to get golden around the edges, about 1 minute. Carefully add tomatoes (beware of splatter!), then 2 1/2 tsp. salt and 1 cup water. Bring sauce to a simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, until slightly reduced, 10–12 minutes. Mix in spinach and cook until wilted, about 2 minutes. Add beans and cook until warmed through, about 3 minutes. Taste ragù and season with more salt if needed.

Meanwhile, heat broiler. Melt butter in a small saucepan over medium heat. Mix in grated garlic and remaining 1/2 tsp. salt and cook until fragrant and sizzling but garlic is not yet browned, about 30 seconds; remove from heat.

Arrange bread, cut side down, on a foil-lined baking sheet and broil until golden brown, about 1 minute. Remove from broiler and turn bread over. Brush untoasted sides of bread with garlic butter and broil until evenly golden brown on top, about 1 minute (keep watch; it will go fast).
Divide toast among plates. Ladle a generous amount of ragù over and top with Parmesan and parsley.
Ragù can be made 3 days ahead. Let cool; cover and chill. Reheat over medium-low.

wheat-tortillas-pequerecetas.name: Wheat Tortillas

ingredients.item: 250 g de harina de trigo (unas 2 tazas)
2 cucharadas de aceite de oliva (30 ml o 2 cucharadas soperas)
(optional) ½ teaspoon of chemical yeast if we want them more fluffy

instructions: In a big bowl, we mix 250 g of wheat flour with 1 teaspoon of salt. If we want to have more tender, we also add ½ teaspoons of chemical yeast, but this isn't necessary.
Incorporamos 2 cucharadas de aceite de oliva y 150 ml de agua templada, poco a poco, mientras removemos con una cuchara o las manos.

We amassed for about 5 minutes, until we get a soft and elastic mass that doesn't stick to our hands. If it's too dry, we add one or two more tablespoons of water and if it's too wet, a little more flour.
We form a ball and cover it with a clean cloth and let it stand about 15 minutes at room temperature to relax the glutan.
Pasado ese tiempo, dividimos la masa en 8 porciones iguales y formamos bolitas.
We slightly tighten up the work surface and with a roller we extend each ball to form a fine circle about 18 cm in diameter.

We heat up an anti-stick frying pan with no oil at medium-high heat. When hot, we put an omelette and cook it about 30 to 40 seconds on each side, till they start to get bubbling and get slightly golden.
We stackle them into a plate covered with a clean cloth to maintain heat and moisture while we cook the rest.

whipped-shortbread-cookies.name: Whipped Shortbread Cookies
instructions: Preheat the oven to 163°C (165 degrees C). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
Beat flour, butter, and confectioners’ sugar together in a large bowl with an electric mixer on medium speed until very light and fluffy, 8 to 10 minutes; dough should look pale, soft, and airy.
Drop tablespoonfuls of dough 2 inches apart on the prepared baking sheets. Top each cookie with a cherry quarter.
Bake in the preheated oven until bottoms are light golden, 18 to 20 minutes. Cool on the baking sheets for 5 minutes; transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.

whirl-y-gig.name: Whirl-y-gig
instructions: Combine rye whiskey, applejack, lemon juice, simple syrup, and Angostura bitters in a cocktail shaker; fill shaker with ice. Cover and shake until outside of shaker is frosty, about 30 seconds. Pour into a rocks glass filled with ice and top off with club soda. Garnish with a lemon twist.

white-bean-ragout-with-toast.name: White Bean Ragout With Toast
instructions: Pulse onions in a food processor until finely chopped but not puréed (you should have about 2 cups). Transfer to a medium bowl. Pulse bell pepper in processor until finely chopped but not puréed (you should have about 1cup); add to bowl and mix well.

Heat oil in a large heavy skillet over medium heat. Add onion mixture (it may splatter) and season with salt and pepper. Simmer, stirring often, until vegetables are completely softened, about 30 minutes. Add finely grated garlic and tomato paste and cook, stirring often, until tomato paste begins to turn deep red, about 3 minutes. Measure 1/2 cup soffritto and set aside; reserve skillet. Transfer remaining soffritto to a container and let cool completely, uncovered. Cover and store in refrigerator for up to 4 days or freeze for up to 3 months.

Meanwhile, preheat oven to 375°. Rub bread slices with cut sides of remaining garlic clove. Place bread on a baking sheet and sprinkle 1 Tbsp. Parmesan over each slice. Toast until cheese begins to brown, about 5 minutes. Set aside.

Heat reserved 1/2 cup soffritto and beans in same skillet over medium-high heat. Cook, stirring often, until heated through, about 1 minute. Stir in 3 cups broth; bring to a boil. Simmer, scraping up browned bits, until liquid is slightly thickened, 3–4 minutes. Add tomatoes and remaining 1 cup broth; simmer until tomatoes are tender, 3–4 minutes. Stir in 2 Tbsp. Parmesan. Season with salt and pepper.
Divide bread among bowls. Top with some bean mixture and broth. Garnish with remaining 2 Tbsp. Parmesan and parsley. Drizzle with oil, if desired.

white-chocolate-chestnut-souffle-recipe-.name: White Chocolate Chestnut Soufflé Recipe

ingredients.item: 2 mins of powder sugar (for aluminum yellows)
2 mins of powder sugar (for aluminum whites)
30 gr butter + some extra for liquor pots
Milky hazelnut or bruçcan be replaced byikolata.
White can be enjoyed with juicy or nuts polishes.
You can create a different flavor with nut cream, almond paste or dulce de leche.
Ready-made chest pücan be used instead of salt and chestnut.
Margarine or can be replaced by a mild vegetable oil, but the taste of butter increases the flavor of sufle.
Milk instead of milk, peel milk or a creamy texture for half-fat milk + cream mixture can be used.
The mixture of almond flour or gluten-free flour can be used instead of normal flour.
It is difficult to change the egg, but the preparation is white part with aquafaba (nohut water) in vegan recipes instead of eggs.
Instead of white sugar, you can get different tastes with powder brunette sugar or coconut sugar.

white-fruitcake.name: White Fruitcake
instructions: Chop raisins, pineapple chunks, and cherries into smaller pieces and transfer to a large bowl.
Add currants, orange peel, and citron peel to the bowl. Pour in orange juice, cover, and soak, 8 hours to overnight.
Preheat the oven to 135°C (135 degrees C). Place a small pan of water on a lower shelf in the oven. Line one 5x9-inch loaf pan and two 3x8-inch loaf pans with parchment paper.
Beat butter in a large bowl with an electric mixer until creamy. Gradually beat in confectioners' sugar until incorporated.

Whisk egg yolks in a separate bowl until thick and pale in color. Add to the butter mixture and mix until incorporated. Mix in soaked fruit and orange juice mixture, then gradually mix in sifted flour.
In another clean bowl, beat egg whites until peaks form. Fold egg whites into the batter. Fill each of the prepared pans 2/3 full with batter.
Bake in the preheated oven until golden brown and a toothpick comes out clean when inserted in the center, 2 to 2 ½ hours.
